justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Well over 5 failure modes identified — Payment Service unavailable, Stripe slow/unavailable, network timeout/phantom charge, notification silent loss, partial failure/saga, zombie PAYMENT_PENDING orders, circuit breaker tuning, retry amplification. (2) Phantom charge risk explicitly identified as critical with detailed mitigation via idempotency keys and status-check endpoint. (3) Fire-and-forget notification weakness directly addressed with transactional outbox pattern replacement. (4) Circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific placement guidance and layered defense. (5) Idempotency keys specified end-to-end from Order through Payment to Stripe. (6) Trade-offs for added complexity thoroughly acknowledged across three detailed trade-off analyses.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough failure mode enumeration with 7 distinct risks, each with severity rating and specific mitigation strategy
Correctly identifies phantom charge as critical and provides multi-layered mitigation: idempotency keys + status-check endpoint + reconciliation job
Explicitly calls out retry amplification pitfall (3×3=9 calls) — a subtle but important distributed systems concern
Phased implementation guidance (3 phases) with specific file paths and clear prioritization of high-ROI items first
Bulkhead isolation pattern included — goes beyond the expected circuit breaker/timeout basics
Pitfalls section is excellent — 'DO NOT catch and swallow timeout errors' and 'DO NOT retry without idempotency keys' directly address the rubric's failure modes
Correctly distinguishes transient vs permanent failures through circuit breaker + timeout layering

Implementation guidance mentions specific retry parameters conceptually but doesn't provide the exact backoff values (e.g., 100ms/500ms/1000ms) called for in the rubric — only gives timeout values (5s, 10s)

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"This synchronous-chain architecture (Order→Payment→Stripe) creates a fragile call path where any single failure cascades upstream, while the fire-and-forget notification path silently loses emails. The design needs circuit breakers, idempotency keys, transactional outbox for notifications, and a saga/compensation pattern for the Order-Payment interaction to achieve production-grade resilience.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Order Service","responsibility":"Accepts
